title: Votes and Verdicts: Joyce Adetutu on Tariff Risks, Refunds
summary: Joyce Adetutu, a partner and trade attorney at Vinson & Elkins, joins Bloomberg Intelligence litigation analyst Holly Froum on this episode of the Votes and Verdicts podcast to discuss the status of refunds tied to tariffs the Supreme Court found unlawful in February. They explore how a potential appeal by the US government could jeopardize importers’ refund rights, new tariffs that may be on the horizon, the strength of potential legal challenges to those tariffs and more.

title: Mol Shares Drop After Petrochemical Plant Blast in Hungary
summary: Shares in Mol Nyrt., Hungary’s biggest energy group, dropped after an accidental explosion at its petrochemicals plant in eastern Hungary killed one person and injured nine others.

title: S&P 500 on Track for Strongest Earnings Growth Since 2021
summary: The S&P 500 Index is on track for its strongest earnings growth in five years as momentum spreads beyond artificial intelligence-fueled gains to much of corporate America.

title: Adecco CEO Warns of Hit From Swiss 10 Million Population Cap
summary: Staffing company Adecco Group AG has spoken out against a proposal to cap Switzerland’s population at 10 million, warning that restrictions on labor movement would curb economic growth and even harm society.

title: Itausa, GIC and Equipav Said to Create Group to Bid for Copasa
summary: Itausa SA, the Singapore sovereign wealth fund GIC and Equipav Saneamento SA are planning to jointly bid to become strategic investors in the privatization process for Copasa, the sanitation company owned by the government of Minas Gerais, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: BofA’s Hartnett Warns Mega-IPOs Risk Bubble Like Roaring ‘20s
summary: Mega IPOs like those eyed by SpaceX and OpenAI threaten to push the weighting of tech in equity benchmarks beyond market-bubble levels of concentration, according to Bank of America Corp. strategist Michael Hartnett.

title: Turkish Assets Bounce Back After Court Blow to Opposition
summary: Turkish assets rebounded after a rout yesterday as investors shrugged off the fallout from a court decision removing the leadership of the country’s main opposition party.

title: Oil Pares Gain With Market Swinging on US-Iran Peace Prospects
summary: Oil pared an earlier gain as prices continue to swing sharply on expectations around a peace deal between the US and Iran.

title: AI-Fueled Rally Puts S&P 500 on Track for Eighth Weekly Gain
summary: US equity futures rose as investors struck an upbeat tone ahead of a long holiday weekend, with optimism fueled by hopes for resolution of hostilities in the Middle East, resilient economic data and relentless enthusiasm for artificial intelligence-linked trades.

title: US Stocks Eye Best Weekly Run of Gains Since 2023: Markets Wrap
summary: The S&P 500 is heading for its longest streak of weekly wins since 2023 as hopes for progress toward a peace deal in the Middle East and unrelenting enthusiasm for artificial intelligence keep fueling global stocks.

title: India RBI Dividend Trails Estimates, Clouds Deficit Goal
summary: India’s central bank will transfer a record yet smaller-than-expected dividend to the government this year, limiting fiscal support as soaring energy prices put public finances under strain.

title: Mexico Mid-May Inflation Slowed After Banxico Ended Easing Cycle
summary: Mexico’s annual inflation slowed in line with expectations in early May, after the central bank late last month delivered a final interest rate cut of a two-year easing cycle.

title: US Blockade Is Painful for Iran, Sanders Says
summary: Col. Wayne Sanders (Ret.), of Bloomberg Intelligence, says the US blockade of Iran is causing some pain and does give President Donald Trump some leverage. He speaks on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Saudi PIF Considers Creating a Logistics Champion
summary: The Saudi wealth fund is looking to combine its ports, rail and shipping holdings into a logistics giant. Bloomberg's Omar El Chmouri reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Edges Down as Traders Weigh Prospects of US-Iran Peace Deal
summary: Gold moved in a narrow range as conflicting signals on the progress of US-Iran ceasefire talks continued to keep traders guessing over the outlook for interest rates and the dollar.

title: Kevin Warsh to Be Sworn In as Fed Chair Today
summary: Kevin Warsh is set to be sworn into office as the 17th chair of the Federal Reserve, taking over at a tense moment for the economy and the central bank. Krishna Guha, Evercore ISI vice chair and head of central bank strategy, talks about how Warsh will manage monetary policy.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Musk’s Latest Venture Gives Space Stocks Their Own Tesla Surge
summary: With the highly-anticipated debut of SpaceX on the US stock market around the corner, euphoria for Elon Musk’s rocket venture is drawing comparisons to Tesla Inc.’s effect on electric-vehicle stocks.

title: Kayne Anderson Raises $5 Billion for Latest Real Estate Fund
summary: Kayne Anderson Capital Advisors’ real estate arm has raised $5.12 billion for its largest-ever opportunistic fund, blowing past its original target of $3 billion amid booming demand for healthcare and consumer-related services.

title: Inside Zara: Inditex CEO Exclusive on Fashion & AI | The Pulse 5/22/2026
summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests:
Clemens Fuest, Ifo Institute, President; Wei Li, BlackRock, Global Chief Investment Strategist; Eléonore Caroit, French Government, Deputy Foreign Minister;  Óscar García Maceiras, Inditex, CEO.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Deutsche, JPMorgan See Weaker Canadian Dollar as Inflation Cools
summary: Deutsche Bank and JPMorgan are piling up on wagers that the Canadian dollar will continue to fall in 2026 as the country’s tame inflation readings fuel a rethink of the Bank of Canada’s interest-rate hike outlook.

title: US Premarket Movers for May 22, 2026
summary: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.2% as of 7:50 a.m. in New York, with stocks heading toward their longest streak of weekly gains since 2023.

title: Mercuria Gets October UK Trial in Multibillion Dollar Hormuz Spat
summary: Commodity trader Mercuria Energy Group Ltd. was granted an early trial over an alleged distortion to the world’s benchmark oil tanker rate that it argues is costing the company hundreds of millions of dollars.

title: Only in Argentina: Junk-Rated Bonds Yield Less Than US Treasuries
summary: For all of President Javier Milei’s libertarian reforms, there are still all sorts of byzantine financial rules in place, like restrictions on lending and moving money overseas, that are creating distortions in Argentina’s capital markets.

title: Germany Confirms Decision to Buy 40% Stake in Tankmaker KNDS
summary: The German government confirmed that it wants to acquire a 40% stake in KNDS NV for several billion euros before the tankmaker’s planned initial public offering this summer.

title: Investec Seeks Ireland Banking License as Lender Eyes EU Bounty
summary: Investec has applied for a banking license in Ireland as it seeks to push into Europe to tap one of the world’s largest financial markets.

title: Inditex Celebrates 25th Anniversary of IPO
summary:
Customers are increasingly demanding 'experiential' retail interactions when it comes to buying clothes, whether that be online or in-store says Óscar García Maceiras, CEO of retail conglomerate and Zara owner Inditex. 

In an exclusive interview, he discussed 25 years of Inditex being a publicly traded company, the value of being a family-owned company, the use of AI in retail, and the importance of diversification both geographically and across product lines. 

He spoke on "The Pulse with Francine Lacqua" on Bloomberg TV. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bellwether Industrial Metal Copper Is Trading Like an AI Stock
summary: Copper, famed for tracking shifts in the global industrial economy, is trading like a high-flying tech stock as investors bet that skyrocketing power use for artificial intelligence will fuel a surge in demand.

title: China Launches Major Crackdown on Illegal Securities Trading
summary: China has launched an unprecedented campaign against illegal cross-border trading to stem capital outflows, threatening severe penalties against popular brokers and ordering non-compliant accounts to be liquidated within two years.

title: US Stocks Eye Longest Stretch of Weekly Gains Since 2023 | Bloomberg Brief 5/22/2026
summary: The Pentagon weighs scrapping a conditional loan offer to rare-earths refiner ReElement Technologies amid an agreement that was meant to help break China's dominance on critical minerals. Shares of Spanish retail group Puig Brands drag as merger talks with Estee Lauder falls through. Priya Misra of JPMorgan Asset Management discussed the economic implications of a prolonged conflict in the Middle East.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Big Oil’s Bumper Iran Profits Become a Juicy Tax Target for Europe
summary: A number of countries are looking at ways to get extra revenue from energy companies.

title: EM Stocks Head for Weekly Gain on AI Rally and Iran Hopes
summary: Emerging-market stocks extended gains for a second day as investors kept piling into artificial intelligence-related stocks and optimism grew that the US and Iran may be moving closer to a peace deal.
